system_content.task: assess the importance of each research task phase based on the paper title and abstract, assigning a ranking, identifying tools/methods used, and additionally, determining whether the described tools/methods involve AI methods.

heads.head: For each research task phase in the list, conduct a thorough analysis of the paper and provide a comprehensive assessment including the following fields for each phase

properties.content: Detailed description of how this phase is addressed in the research, including specific methodologies, approaches, and key activities
properties.ranking: Importance ranking from 1 to 3 (where 1 means minimal importance/contribution and 3 means critical importance/contribution, with 3 representing the greatest relative importance to the overall paper)
properties.tools_methods: A coherent paragraph describing the tools, techniques, software, instruments, and methods used in this phase, explaining how they were applied and their role in the research process
properties.ai_methods_judgment:
Based on the description provided in the 'tools_methods' field, determine whether AI methods are explicitly mentioned or clearly implied, 'yes' or 'no'. This judgment must adhere strictly to the following rules:
        - Set "use_ai_methods" to "yes" only if AI methods are explicitly mentioned or clearly implied in the tools/methods description.
        - If "use_ai_methods" is "yes", list the names of all identified AI methods (e.g., "Deep Learning", "Convolutional Neural Networks") in the "ai_methods_list". Do NOT provide explanations for each method.
        - If no AI methods are mentioned or implied, set "use_ai_methods" to "no" and set "ai_methods_list" to an empty array [].
        - Only include AI methods that are directly supported by the tools/methods description for that specific phase.

heads.head: Ensure all descriptions are specific, detailed, and directly supported by evidence from the title and abstract
Strictly return the result only in the following JSON format (without any other text)

data_and_material_collection_and_preproc.content: The study collected 2,500 multi-center MRI scans from five medical institutions over 24 months. Preprocessing included DICOM to NIfTI conversion, intensity normalization using N4 bias field correction, skull stripping with FSL BET, and spatial normalization to MNI space. Data augmentation was applied through rotation, scaling, and elastic deformation. Quality control involved independent review by two radiologists.
data_and_material_collection_and_preproc.tools_methods: Data acquisition utilized 3T Siemens and Philips Ingenia MRI scanners with standardized protocols. DICOM to NIfTI conversion was performed using dcm2niix. Intensity normalization used N4 bias field correction in ANTs. Skull stripping employed FSL BET algorithm. Spatial normalization utilized advanced normalization tools (ANTs) to MNI space. Data augmentation was implemented through MONAI framework. Quality control procedures were managed via custom Python scripts and REDCap platform.

modeling_or_analysis_framework_construct.content: A comprehensive deep learning framework was developed featuring five CNN architectures (ResNet-50, DenseNet-121, EfficientNet-B4, Inception-v3, custom 3D CNN) with transfer learning from ImageNet and ensemble methods. Hyperparameter optimization used Bayesian methods with 200+ experiments. The framework included both individual model training and ensemble approaches.
modeling_or_analysis_framework_construct.tools_methods: The modeling framework was built using PyTorch and TensorFlow. Transfer learning utilized ImageNet pre-trained weights. Experimental management used Weights & Biases platform. Hyperparameter optimization employed Optuna library with Bayesian optimization. Computational resources included NVIDIA DGX-2 cluster. Reproducibility was ensured through Docker containers and MLflow for model versioning. Custom data loaders were developed for medical imaging formats.

data_analysis_and_interpretation.content: Statistical analysis included calculation of accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC-ROC with 95% confidence intervals using bootstrapping. Subgroup analysis by tumor type, demographics, and imaging center. Statistical significance testing with Bonferroni correction. Clinical relevance assessed through decision curve analysis and comparison with radiologist performance.
data_analysis_and_interpretation.tools_methods: Statistical analysis used Scikit-learn for metrics calculation and bootstrapping via custom Python scripts. Statistical testing employed SciPy library. Some analyses used R environment. Data visualization used Matplotlib and Seaborn. Model interpretability used SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations). Clinical relevance assessment employed decision curve analysis methodology.
